title: Chicken Katsu
description: Crispy Japanese breaded chicken cutlets served with tonkatsu sauce. A family favorite that's easier than you think!
instructions: Pound chicken breasts or thighs to 0.5 inch thickness
Season chicken with salt on both sides
Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs mixed with water, one with panko breadcrumbs
Coat each chicken piece in flour, shaking off excess
Dip in egg mixture, then press firmly into panko breadcrumbs
Heat oil in a 10-inch pan to 325-350°F
Carefully place breaded chicken in hot oil
Fry for 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through
Remove to paper towels to drain
Make tonkatsu sauce by mixing ketchup, Worcestershire, oyster sauce, miso, and honey
Serve chicken with tonkatsu sauce on the side
tips: Pound chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't skip the egg wash - it helps breadcrumbs stick
Press panko firmly so it adheres well
Oil temperature is crucial - use a thermometer
Serve with rice and steamed vegetables
Tonkatsu sauce can be made ahead
